title: Resource tagging and labeling standard
summary: The canonical org-wide tag/label taxonomy every cloud resource and k8s object on the nanohype stack carries. Defines vendor-neutral dimensions, their per-surface rendering (AWS/Azure tag keys, GCP labels, k8s labels, OTel attributes), the deterministic casing transforms, the reserved namespaces, and which dimensions are required. cloudgov reads the required-tier keys from this file to gate CI; landing-zone and the eks-agent-platform operator inject them; tenant charts label workloads from it.

transforms.aws: Render the canonical kebab-case id as PascalCase (split on '-', capitalize each part, join). cost-center -> CostCenter.
transforms.azure: Same as aws (PascalCase). Azure tag keys are case-insensitive with a broad charset; reuse the AWS rendering for cross-cloud consistency.
transforms.gcp_key: Render the canonical id as snake_case (replace '-' with '_'; the id is already lowercase). cost-center -> cost_center.
transforms.gcp_value: Sanitize the value: lower-case it, replace '-' with '_', replace '/' with '_', truncate to 63 chars. GCP label values accept only [a-z0-9_-]. nanohype/landing-zone -> nanohype_landing_zone.
transforms.k8s: Well-known dimensions render under app.kubernetes.io/<leaf>; org dimensions render under <group>.nanohype.dev/<id>. The label value must match [a-z0-9A-Z._-]{0,63}.
transforms.otel: Dotted lower-case attribute names. Reserved namespace agents.* (tenant/platform/model identity) plus the well-known deployment.environment and service.version.

dimensions.meaning: Deploy stage: dev, staging, production, or org.
The tool that owns the resource lifecycle. Value: opentofu (substrate) or eks-agent-platform (operator-reconciled).
The substrate project the resource belongs to. Value: landing-zone.
The source repository as org/name (e.g. nanohype/landing-zone).
Data sensitivity: public, internal, confidential, or restricted.
Compliance regime: soc2, hipaa, or none.
The substrate component or workload role (network, cluster, llm, ...).
The owning team that operates the resource (e.g. platform).
Accountable handle for escalation (a team or GitHub-team slug). Auto-filled from the env-level owner, defaulting to team when unset; never hand-entered per resource.
The deployed source revision (a short git SHA or chart appVersion) for provenance and rollback correlation.
The factory job or CI run that created the resource (a pipeline run id). Feeds provenance and attribution.
Whether the resource is reapable: ephemeral or persistent. Ephemeral resources past their expiry are cleanup candidates.
An ISO-8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) after which an ephemeral resource is safe to reap. Set by the vend layer as now + ttl; omit on persistent resources.
The Platform tenant a per-tenant resource belongs to. Applies only to multi-tenant components and per-tenant cloud resources.
The Platform CR name (the app). k8s and OTel only — never a cloud tag.
The LLM family for AI workloads. OTel only — runtime cost attribution.
The full Bedrock model id for AI workloads. OTel only — runtime cost attribution.
